name: OmopObservationCodedView
title: Observation → measurement / observation (FHIR-flat, stage 1; per-coding fan-out)
description:
Stage 1 of FHIR→OMOP. One staging row per `Observation.code.coding[]` entry (per-coding fan-out) instead of one row per Observation. Carries the UNION of measurement + observation value-slots so a single canonical staging table (staging.observation_coded) feeds both sibling edges.

Stage 2 resolves each coding through cm.fhir_system_to_omop_vocab + Maps-to ONCE in mapspec/etl/_resolve_observation.sql, dedups when two codings of the same Observation resolve to the same standard concept_id, and routes by std.domain_id to the appropriate OMOP table (measurement / observation). The sibling stage-2 SQLs (Observation__measurement / Observation__observation) are then trivial `WHERE std_domain='…'` filters over staging.observation_resolved — same shape as the Condition family.

Diff page caveat: the reference Synthea CSV (cdm.* oracle) carries one CODE per observation row, so per-coding fan-out can add rows our side. Diff joins on observation_source_value (the raw LOINC code) so it stays meaningful.

column.name: id
column.description: FHIR Observation.id; → surrogate via stringToId(id || '|' || std_concept_id) in stage 2 (fan-out safe).

column.name: code_text
column.description: Free-text label → fallback *_source_value when no coding resolves.

column.name: value_string
column.description: Plain string value (OMOP observation only) → value_as_string.

column.name: value_code_system
column.description: When value[x] is a CodeableConcept: system of first coding → value_as_concept_id via Maps-to.

column.name: range_low
column.description: First reference range lower bound → range_low (measurement only).

column.name: range_high
column.description: First reference range upper bound → range_high (measurement only).
